title: Vector Spaces & Dimensions
concept: High-dimensional spaces represent datasets where each feature acts as a completely new, perpendicular direction or axis in space.

exercises.prompt: Recap: what does multiplying a data matrix by a weight vector produce?

options.text: A vector of predictions for every example in the matrix
A single number representing the average error

exercises.explain: Right — it computes predictions in a single batch. Now, where do these vectors actually live?

cards.title: Beyond the 3D world
cards.body: We live in a 3D world: up/down, left/right, forward/back. But in ML, a dataset with 50 features lives in a 50-dimensional space, where every feature is a new direction.

cards.title: Dimensions and spaces
cards.body: A dimension is an axis of measurement. A vector space is the mathematical arena where our vectors live, where each feature is a completely new direction perpendicular to all others.

cards.title: Adding dimensions to a map
cards.body: A flat paper map is 2D (North/South, East/West). A globe is 3D (adding height). In ML, we just keep adding new axes — like humidity, wind, and pressure — to build a high-dimensional weather space.

cards.title: A 5-dimensional patient space
cards.body: A patient vector [age, heart_rate, temperature, weight, blood_pressure] sits in a 5D vector space. Each patient is a single dot positioned in this 5D room.

cards.title: You don't need to visualize it
cards.body: Don't try to picture a 10-dimensional shape — human brains can't! Just treat it as 10 separate sliders. The math works exactly the same in 100 dimensions as it does in 2.

left.text:
2 features = 2 perpendicular axes
e.g. Size vs. Price

right.text:
10 features = 10 perpendicular axes
represented as a vector of length 10

exercises.prompt: What does a dimension represent in a machine learning vector space?

options.text: A feature or axis of measurement
The length of the training loop

exercises.explain: Each feature is a dimension (axis) in our vector space, letting us place data points in a coordinate system.

exercises.prompt: True or false: the mathematical equations for vectors only work in 2D and 3D spaces.
exercises.explain: False — the math works identically across any number of dimensions, from 2 to 20,000.

exercises.sentence: A dataset with 10 features has vectors that live in a ___-dimensional vector space.
exercises.explain: Since each feature adds one dimension, 10 features occupy a 10-dimensional space.

exercises.prompt: You represent a customer using 100 features. Where does this customer's vector live?
exercises.explain: A vector of 100 numbers represents a point in a 100-dimensional space.

exercises.prompt: A house price model adds 3 new features (school rating, crime rate, age) to its existing 2 features (size, bedrooms). What happens to the mathematical space where the house vectors live?

options.text: It expands from a 2D space to a 5D space, adding 3 new perpendicular axes of measurement
It stays a 2D space but the numbers get larger
It becomes a matrix of shape 3x2

exercises.explain: Outstanding! Every new feature is a brand new perpendicular direction, expanding the coordinate system. You have successfully finished the Linear Algebra for ML foundations unit! Next up: exploring supervised learning!
